mnemonics: M.T. Locker: Multiple Threads need a Locker to safely share data.
keyPoints: Thread safety ensures that data remains consistent when accessed by multiple threads concurrently.
Common techniques include synchronization, atomic operations, and immutable objects.
A lack of thread safety can lead to race conditions, deadlocks, and corrupted data.
commonMistakes: Assuming a block of code is thread-safe without proper analysis or synchronization.
Using synchronization incorrectly, leading to performance bottlenecks or deadlocks.
